creator: pyunyh@gmail.com
text:
On Tue, Dec 19, 2006 at 10:10:29PM +0000, Helmut Schellong wrote:
 > The following reply was made to PR kern/105375; it has been noted by GNATS.
 > 
 > From: Helmut Schellong <var@schellong.biz>
 > To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
 > Cc:  
 > Subject: Re: kern/105375: [sk] 2 x Marvell GBit LAN: device sk0 is attached,
 >  but sk1 not.
 > Date: Tue, 19 Dec 2006 22:55:19 +0100
 >

 >           The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.
 >  FreeBSD is a registered trademark of The FreeBSD Foundation.
 >  FreeBSD 6.2-RC1 #0: Fri Nov 17 19:04:45 CET 2006
 >       root@bsd.lokal.biz:/usr/src/sys/i386/compile/BSD62SMP
 >  Timecounter "i8254" frequency 1193182 Hz quality 0
 >  CPU: Intel(R) Core(TM)2 CPU          6600  @ 2.40GHz (2400.01-MHz 686-class CPU)
 >     Origin = "GenuineIntel"  Id = 0x6f6  Stepping = 6
 >   

[...]

 >  pcib4: <ACPI PCI-PCI bridge> irq 17 at device 28.5 on pci0
 >  pci2: <ACPI PCI bus> on pcib4
 >  pci2: <network, ethernet> at device 0.0 (no driver attached)
    ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

Ok, I think this is the NIC you want to use.
Please show me "pciconf -lv" output.

[...]

 >  skc0: <Marvell Gigabit Ethernet> port 0xc800-0xc8ff mem 0xfeaf4000-0xfeaf7fff irq 19 at device 4.0 
 >  on pci5
 >  skc0: Marvell Yukon Lite Gigabit Ethernet rev. (0x9)
 >  sk0: <Marvell Semiconductor, Inc. Yukon> on skc0
 >  sk0: Ethernet address: 00:17:31:69:a0:77
 >  miibus0: <MII bus> on sk0
 >  e1000phy0: <Marvell 88E1000 Gigabit PHY> on miibus0
 >  e1000phy0:  10baseT, 10baseT-FDX, 100baseTX, 100baseTX-FDX, 1000baseTX-FDX, auto

[...]
-- 
Regards,
Pyun YongHyeon
